user_query:
I need a high-signal implementation brief on progressive profiling for onboarding forms using my indexed notes. Please read all markdown files under input/notes/ and do this:

1) Use a two-stage search approach: first a fast keyword pass, then a semantic pass, then re-rank and deduplicate the combined results. Capture the final top 5 documents in output/search_results.json with the following schema per item:
- path: relative path under input/notes/
- method: one of "keyword", "semantic", or "hybrid" (use at least one keyword and one semantic item across the 5)
- rank: integer 1..5 with no duplicates
- relevance_reason: 1–2 sentences explaining why it’s relevant

2) Write output/brief.md containing these sections: Summary, Implementation Plan, Pitfalls, Citations. In the Summary, explain the two-stage search and how you re-ranked and de-duplicated results. In Implementation Plan, give concrete steps for how to implement progressive profiling in onboarding (fields to ask first vs later, UI patterns, data handling). In Pitfalls, discuss conversion friction and compliance/privacy risks. In Citations, include at least 5 direct quotes (one per line) from the input notes in the format: "- [input/notes/<filename>.md:line N] "<verbatim quote>"". Include at least one quote sourced from input/notes/privacy_considerations.md and at least one from input/notes/form_performance.md. Do not invent content—quote only what exists in input/notes/.

3) Create output/full_docs/ and write exact copies of the two most relevant documents you selected (same basenames as in input/notes/). Keep all paths relative (no absolute paths).

Deliverables to create:
- output/search_results.json
- output/brief.md
- output/full_docs/<two files>

If something seems missing, state your assumptions in the Summary section. Avoid any visual media.
